column1: What are the essential requirements for an encryption system to function?
column2: Data to encrypt, tools and processes to perform encryption, and a defined encryption algorithm

column1: What are the two main layers in Key Management Infrastructure (KMI)?
column2: Storage layer (responsible for storing encryption keys) and Management layer (allows authorized users to access stored keys)

column1: What are three valid approaches for managing encryption keys in AWS?
column2: Use third-party HSM for storage with customer-managed processes, fully manage keys and KMI yourself, or use AWS KMS which supports both encryption and KMI

column1: In server-side encryption with KMS (SSE-KMS), who manages the encryption keys?
What types of encryption do both KMS and CloudHSM support?
What encryption algorithm does AWS KMS use?
When an organization wants AWS to fully manage encryption keys without any customer management, which S3 encryption option should they use?
What feature allows KMS to store keys in a CloudHSM cluster?
What is the simplest AWS solution for encrypting an EBS volume with minimal development time?
Does KMS integrate with common AWS services?
column2: Yes, KMS integrates with services like S3, EBS, and other AWS services

column1: What is the key difference between SSE-S3 and SSE-KMS?
column2: SSE-S3: S3 manages all encryption keys and rotations automatically; SSE-KMS: KMS manages the keys and customers can create customer KMS keys

column1: What happens with SSE-C (Server-Side Encryption with Customer-provided keys)?
column2: The customer provides the key to the S3 service but S3 still performs the encryption server-side

column1: Why might an organization choose CloudHSM over standard KMS?
column2: To maintain greater control over key storage while still integrating with KMS through custom key stores

column1: What does the storage layer in KMI do?
What does the management layer in KMI do?
column2: Controls and manages authorized user access to the stored encryption keys
